intro.title: Reproducibility Concepts
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Reproducibility Concepts is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode: print("Same data + code + environment => same results")

challenges.title: Easy: Use print
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `print` related to **Reproducibility Concepts**.
challenges.starterCode: print("Same data + code + environment => same results")

tests.label: Correct output
tests.assertion: identical(trimws(_cq_stdout), "Same data + code + environment => same results")

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the number `100` using `print()`.

challenges.title: Easy: Assign a variable
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: Apply Reproducibility Concepts
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write code demonstrating **Reproducibility Concepts**.
challenges.starterCode: print("Same data + code + environment => same results")

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `print` and `print` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **Reproducibility Concepts** in a short script using `print`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **Reproducibility Concepts** (include `print`).
challenges.starterCode: print("Same data + code + environment => same results")

questions.prompt: Reproducible research means:

choices.label: Others can recreate your results from your materials
Results change each run by design

questions.prompt: Which function prints output in R?
R is widely used for:
What assignment operator is preferred in R?
Missing values in R are represented by:
Which function combines values into a vector?
Data frames are:
Type the function to get help on a topic.
Expert: Vectorized operations in R:
Expert: Name one keyword from Reproducibility Concepts.

challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: Apply Publishing Reports
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write code demonstrating **Publishing Reports**.

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `HTML` and `PDF` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **Publishing Reports** in a short script using `HTML`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **Publishing Reports** (include `HTML`).
challenges.starterCode: # Render to HTML/PDF and share via GitHub Pages or Posit Connect
